decisions.title: Ship CSRS (Channelstore Rewrite Service) to production
decisions.context: CSRS work is accelerating (ENG-10157 dedup, ENG-9987 reads, ENG-10160 forwarding, ENG-9989 tests). This is core infrastructure modernization that enables better performance and the 'Build the Moat' pillar. Need to decide go-live timing vs risk.

arguments.for: Integration tests expanding, read forwarding implemented, dedup working. Delays compound technical debt. Moat pillar depends on modern data layer.
arguments.against: Production data risk high. TLS not yet enabled (ENG-10174). Need more soak time. CSV export bug (ENG-10022) shows data path fragility.

decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 (E2) GA over new feature development
decisions.context: E2 launch prep visible: ENG-9779 feature flag audit, ENG-10177 alignment defaults missing, ENG-9537 family edit flows. But also seeing Canvas/Reactor work (ENG-10179) and React 19 upgrade (ENG-6257). 'Nail the Core' means E2 must ship clean.

arguments.for: E2 is the primary vehicle for Decisions Captured. Family features (ENG-10105, ENG-10106) and E2 polish directly serve North Star. React upgrade can wait.
arguments.against: Reactor backend (ENG-10179) serves 'Embed Deeper'. React 19 unblocks performance gains. Parallel workstreams show team capacity exists.

decisions.title: Define 'Decision Captured' metric instrumentation before May 1
decisions.context: North Star is Decisions Captured Per Customer but no instrumentation tickets visible. Ghost events bug (ENG-9978), report graph issues (ENG-9979), and UDF dependency display bug (ENG-10006) suggest analytics/observability gaps. Can't manage what we don't measure.
decisions.blockedBy: No defined schema for what constitutes a 'decision' in product telemetry. E2 GA must ship with instrumentation.

arguments.for: Without measurement, strategy is theater. Q2 ends June 30—need 6 weeks of clean data to assess. Rules events, family creation, Canvas saves are decision proxies.
arguments.against: Premature optimization. Let E2 usage patterns emerge organically first, then instrument. Team already stretched on delivery.

decisions.title: Sunset feature flags older than 6 months as policy
decisions.context: ENG-7446 proposes improving feature flag interface. ENG-9779 is E2 GA audit. ENG-10182 enables enum flags. Proliferation of flags creates technical debt and slows 'Nail the Core'. Need cleanup discipline.

arguments.for: Flags are temporary scaffolding, not architecture. 6-month rule forces ship-or-kill decisions. Reduces test matrix, improves velocity, aligns with 'moat' pillar.
arguments.against: Some features need long beta periods. Customer-specific flags provide flexibility. Forced cleanup may rush quality decisions.

decisions.title: Commit to Files in CEL beta launch date
decisions.context: ENG-10170 shows pre-beta fixes in flight. Files in CEL could be a Python Rules alternative (serving 'Embed Deeper'). But no clear ship date. Ask SIFT and Python Rules need strategic clarity on file handling story.

arguments.for: Pre-beta fixes suggest imminent readiness. Customer demand for file analysis is proven. Differentiated capability vs competitors (moat).
arguments.against: Scope creep risk—files are complex. Python Rules may be better long-term answer. Don't fragment the roadmap before E2 GA ships.

keyResults.name: Explore V2 GA shipped with <5% critical bug rate

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy engineering execution week. 30 active tickets span infrastructure (CSRS, Redpanda TLS), E2 polish, and new capabilities (Reactor, Files in CEL). Risk: fragmentation across too many initiatives before E2 GA.
alerts: No North Star metric instrumentation work visible—can't track strategy without data
3 data correctness bugs active (CSV, ghost events, UDF dependencies)—quality signal
CSRS migration has 5 active tickets but no visible rollout plan or gate criteria
E2 GA feature flag audit (ENG-9779) suggests imminent launch but 6 polish tickets still open
